title: Sonilo signs Shutterstock deal to train its ‘video-to-music’ AI - Music Ally
text:
What’s a video-to-music AI when it’s at home, you may be wondering? It’s a model that generates music based on a video input rather than a text prompt – the idea being to create a soundtrack that matches the footage.
Sonilo
is one of the startups building such a service, and now it has signed a licensing deal with a big player in the digital media world: Shutterstock.
Sonilo will be training its model on Shutterstock’s music library, having
already opened up its service to video creators in May
promising that its outputs were “production-ready and cleared for commercial use”.
“While others have chosen to take artists’ work without permission and charge creators for the privilege, we’ve chosen a different path – one where artists are compensated from day one,” said CEO Shawn Song.
That’s a good thing, although platforms like Sonilo also pose a challenge for artists, since its outputs are competing with their work when creators are deciding whether to license commercial music or whip up an AI track for a video.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
